signals.title: NZD/JPY — G10 Bilateral Theme + RBNZ Hike Bias
signals.text: NZD/JPY at 92.41 — NZD leads G10 performance while JPY remains the primary laggard. Despite a -0.83% real rate, NZD is supported by a hawkish RBNZ bias (hike priced for 8 July) and a +335bp OIS carry advantage over JPY. JPY COT LF net short -115,033 (28.4% long) reflects crowded carry positioning, while NZD CESI (+7) confirms data outperformance. The 1.258% G10 spread signals a dominant bilateral trend.
signals.watch_level: RBNZ policy decision on 8 July determines if the NZD outperformance persists.

signals.title: USD/CAD — GDP Surprise + Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.4207 — Canada April GDP beat at 0.5% vs forecasts, yet May guidance remains soft, limiting CAD recovery. COT LF net short -87,096 (20.6% long) shows institutional bearishness, while retail sentiment is 94% short (extreme). This institutional vs retail divergence at the 1.42 level suggests retail is fading the trend; a break higher triggers a retail capitulation squeeze. USD CESI (+290) vs CAD CESI (-5) supports the 150bp Fed-BoC carry advantage.
signals.watch_level: Watch for a sustained break above 1.4250 to confirm retail capitulation.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Volatility Premium + Technical Break
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.6913 — Buyers are testing the 100-hour MA following hawkish Fed commentary from Hammack. AUD/USD IV at 17.9% vs HV30 at 7.3% creates a massive +10.6% VRP, the highest in G10, signaling the options market is pricing significant tail risk. Despite a 60bp carry advantage for AUD, the 25d RR at -0.97 shows a persistent put skew as traders hedge against USD strength. COT LF net long +29,035 (64.9% long) suggests specs are already positioned for the bounce.
signals.watch_level: US Nonfarm Payrolls on 3 July is the next volatility catalyst for the 0.6900 handle.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry vs Options Skew + Correlation Break
signals.text: USD/JPY at 162.61 — The 275bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage keeps the pair bid despite a moderate put skew (25d RR -1.23) signaling intervention hedging. The USD/JPY vs US 10Y correlation has broken (z = -1.79σ), with JPY failing to track the rise in US yields to 4.40%. VIX at 16.7 (normal volatility) suggests carry remains the dominant regime driver over safe-haven flows until cross-asset stress intensifies.
signals.watch_level: BoJ meeting on 31 July determines if the TONA 0.98% rate floor shifts.
